User uses a specific JSON structure called Lollipop JSON to represent web pages.
Lollipop JSON to represent web pages
Lollipop JSON has two top-level nodes: 'elements' and 'workflows'.
A text element in Lollipop JSON has properties including text, height, left, top, width, zindex, type 'Text', id, default_name, and style.

attrs.value: properties include text, height, left, top, width, zindex, type, id, default_name, style

attrs.value: properties include text, height, left, top, width, zindex, type, id, default_name, style

label: A shape element in Lollipop JSON has properties including height, left, top, width, zindex, border_roundness, bgcolor, type 'Shape', id, and default_name.

attrs.value: properties include height, left, top, width, zindex, border_roundness, bgcolor, type, id, default_name

label: height, left, top, width, zindex, border_roundness, bgcolor, type, id, default_name
A workflow in Lollipop JSON has properties including element_id, type (e.g., ButtonClicked), id, and actions. Actions have properties including element_id, type (e.g., HideElement), and id.
